rows.brecha: Prompt and bridge still decide sales flow, documents and quote-related copy.
rows.riesgo: Incoherent replies when prompt memory conflicts with tool/state evidence.

rows.brecha: Multiple sources infer state and field names are mixed.
rows.riesgo: Repeated questions or writes to the wrong field alias.

rows.brecha: No single mandatory catalog.retrieve contract across all paths.
rows.riesgo: Vague model references can become unsafe state or wrong quote.

rows.brecha: Dinamo plan order, aliases and down payments are hardcoded globally in core.
rows.riesgo: Tenant leakage, stale commercial rules, hard-to-audit plan decisions.

rows.brecha: Quote contract name/result authority is not uniform.
rows.riesgo: Cash/credit mixing or stale quote in legacy paths.

rows.brecha: Must still be mandatory over FAQ/prompt when plan is known.
rows.riesgo: Generic documents if FAQ/prompt answers first.

rows.brecha: No single mandatory document.check contract owns accepted/rejected docs.
rows.riesgo: Stage moves or docs-complete claims without evidence.

rows.brecha: v2 StateWriter is strong, but live/legacy path still uses bridge policies and aliases.
rows.riesgo: Unsafe writes if an alternate path bypasses v2 policy.

rows.brecha: Engine exists, but target business trigger names are not fully standardized.
rows.riesgo: Keyword-style workflows or duplicate/early side effects.

rows.brecha: Guard coverage is strong in v2 but distributed in legacy.
rows.riesgo: Unsafe visible text if not routed through v2 guard layer.
Provider failures can still cause fallback-quality issues if not monitored.

rows.brecha: Needs target-contract evals for the new architecture.
rows.riesgo: Passing old evals may not prove tool/state/workflow authority.

rows.brecha: Ensure every new contract emits evidence IDs and trace metadata.
rows.riesgo: Good answer may be hard to audit if tool evidence is missing.

rows.brecha: Must remain until parity/canary are proven.
rows.riesgo: Premature cutover would be dangerous for live sales.

most_important_delta: Mandatory tool contracts must become the shared authority before the prompt can be safely reduced.
